mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in over 60 percent of quarters, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (F-test p > 0.05) relative to single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply-side bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of contraction months, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable federal funds rate indicates real rate compression and delayed monetary tightening, which compresses equity discount rates and drives positive forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in over 65 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains flat, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 550 thousand units, construction activity and related durable goods demand collapse, depressing corporate earnings outlooks and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered quarters, or the predictive IC remains below 0.02 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly improve consumer income expectations and reduce recession probability, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive skew during payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between low federal funds rates and stagnant real GDP growth signals ineffective monetary transmission, prompting equity market repricing and negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5 percent while GDPC1 growth remains under 2 percent annualized, or the interaction term loses significance.
